activity.body: Karen Chen, Discharge Planner at UCSF Mission Bay — booked intro for Thu 2:30 PM.

messages.body:
Hi Karen,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run Family Care SF, a family-owned home-care agency in SF since 2001. We send caregivers into patients' homes after discharge: personal care, companion, fall-risk help, overnights. Caregivers fluent in English, Russian, Tagalog, and Spanish.

What I'd want a UCSF discharge planner to know about us: when a patient's medically ready to go home but the home plan isn't quite safe yet, the agencies that earn their spot are the ones that pick up the phone, give you a clear yes or no on staffing within a couple hours, and can start same-day when it's a fit. That's what we work to do every time.

Happy to send a one-pager, or take 10 minutes by phone if it's easier to vet us before a real case comes up.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Hi Alex — that'd actually be useful. Weekend discharges fall through on us too often. Languages and overnight coverage matter. Thursday afternoon work for a quick call?

Thursday's good. We've got caregivers in English, Russian, Tagalog, and Spanish — overnights and weekends are kind of our thing. 2:30 work for you?

Alex
Confirmed — Thu May 14, 2:30 PM. Calendar invite sent.

activity.body: David Reyes, Elder Law at Reyes & Goldman — "send me your intake packet, I have two clients this week."

messages.body:
Hi David,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run Family Care SF, a family-owned home-care agency here in SF since 2001. Private-duty in-home care: personal care, companion, overnights, fall-risk support.

For your conservatorship and trust clients, what tends to matter most isn't the care plan itself — it's that the caregiver shows up consistently, leaves clean shift notes, and someone calls you before something becomes a problem. We've spent 24 years figuring out how to do that without drama.

Happy to send our one-pager so you have something to look at before a client situation comes up.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Send me your intake packet — I have two clients this week. One in Pacific Heights, one in Forest Hill. Both private pay, both need reliable starts.

activity.body: Priya Anand, Social Worker at Laguna Honda — asked about overnight rates and minimum hours.

thread.subject: Russian and Tagalog caregivers in SF

messages.body:
Hi Priya,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run Family Care SF, a family-owned home-care agency in SF since 2001. Caregivers in patients' homes for personal care, companion, fall-risk help, and overnights.

What's worth knowing about us if you're at Laguna Honda: we have caregivers fluent in Russian, Tagalog, and Spanish, plus English, and we keep weekend and overnight coverage staffed. When a patient's heading home and the language match or the odd-hours coverage is what's hard, that's specifically where we tend to be useful — and we'll always get back to you the same morning, yes or no.

Happy to send a one-pager whenever's useful, or take a quick call so you can vet us before a discharge lands.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Thanks Alex. Two quick questions: what's the overnight rate, and what's your minimum hours per shift? We have a Russian-speaking discharge tomorrow if you can staff it.

activity.body: Dr. Marcus Hall, Geriatrician at One Medical Pacific Heights — asked about coverage area and weekend overnights.

thread.subject: Home-care agency for your referral list

messages.body:
Hi Dr. Hall,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run Family Care SF, a family-owned home-care agency in SF since 2001. Caregivers in older patients' homes for personal care, companion, fall-risk support, and overnights. Not nursing.

I know geriatricians often keep a short list of agencies they refer families to when a patient needs help at home. If you'd consider us for yours, I'd appreciate it. We answer the phone, give straight answers, and only say yes when we can actually staff a case.

Happy to send a one-pager whenever it'd be helpful.

Alex
(415) 225-4948

Hi Alex — got it, thanks for reaching out. Two quick questions before I add anyone to my list: do you cover Pacific Heights / Presidio Heights, and how does weekend overnight coverage usually work for new clients?

activity.body: Linda Okafor, Director at Rhoda Goldman Plaza — "not a fit right now, check back in Q3."

thread.subject: Home-care backup when IL isn't enough

messages.body:
Hi Linda,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run Family Care SF, a family-owned home-care agency in SF since 2001. Private-duty: companion, personal care, overnights, fall-risk support.

When an IL resident at Rhoda Goldman starts to need more help, the family is usually scrambling. The agencies that earn a spot in that moment are the ones who pick up the phone, give a clear yes or no, and have a caregiver started within a day. That's what we work to do.

Happy to be on your shortlist of outside agencies whenever you'd consider it. I can send a one-pager any time.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Hi Alex — appreciate the note. Not a fit right now; we have a preferred list locked in through Q2. Check back in Q3 and I'll take a real look.

activity.body: James Whitcombe, Admissions at Sequoias SF — 30-min call confirmed Mon 10:00 AM.

thread.subject: Private-duty for residents who need more

messages.body:
Hi James,

I'm Alex Girsh — I run Family Care SF, a family-owned home-care agency in SF since 2001. Private-duty for residents who need more support than IL covers: companion, personal care, overnights, fall-risk help.

When a Sequoias resident starts to slip, the family is usually asking your team for vendor names and they want someone responsive — clear yes/no, caregiver started within a day. That's what we work to do, and it's why I'd appreciate being on your shortlist before that conversation comes up.

Happy to send a one-pager or do 15 minutes by phone, whichever's easier to vet us with.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Hi Alex — yes, worth a quick chat. Mon 10 AM PT works for me. 30 minutes. I'll send a Zoom link.

activity.body: Outreach batch sent — 14 contacts across hospitals, attorneys, and senior living.
Rachel Brodsky, Care Manager at Institute on Aging — open to a partnership conversation.

thread.subject: Backup home-care staffing for care plans

messages.body:
Hi Rachel,

I'm Alex Girsh, Family Care SF. Family-owned home-care agency in SF since 2001 — caregivers in homes for personal care, companion, overnights.

For care managers, what tends to matter most is staffing reliability and communication discipline: caregivers who show up consistently, leave clean shift notes, and someone who calls you before a problem becomes a problem. That's most of what we work on, and it's where I'd want to earn the chance to be on your list when one of your plans needs backup staffing.

Happy to send a one-pager whenever's useful.

Alex
(415) 225-4948
Alex — open to a partnership conversation. We have plans that drift because the staffing partner isn't responsive. If you're consistent on communication and weekend coverage, we should talk.

lastWeek.recommendation: Pacing well against the 90-day target (500–700 contacts, 10–20 warm conversations). Priority this week: get back to David and Rachel — same-week follow-ups convert ~2× better than waiting.
